steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the most logical transition. The first sentence establishes that Adnan's painting fuels the poem's exploration of temporality and identity; the next sentence introduces a separate additional fact about the poem — that it reflects on the relationship between poetry and other art forms. The transition must signal addition, not restatement, example, or contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In other words,': signals a paraphrase or restatement of the previous sentence. The second sentence is a new claim, not a reworded version of the first. ✗
  • Choice B — 'For instance,': signals that the sentence provides an example of the preceding idea. The second sentence adds a distinct observation rather than illustrating one already made. ✗
  • Choice C — 'What's more,': signals that the sentence adds a second, separate point on top of the first. That is exactly the relationship here: the poem explores temporality and identity and also reflects on poetry's relationship to other art forms. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Conversely,': signals a contrast or reversal. Nothing in the second sentence contradicts or opposes the first. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
